RepurpOS

Bipolar Disorder

Disease intelligence for Bipolar Disorder: 108 alterations and 389 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

US DALYs474.9KWHO GHE 2021 · all ages
Global DALYs8.07MWHO GHE 2021 · worldwide

Disease overview

Natural products

With trial/literature evidence

Sources: Open Targets, HPO, ClinicalTrials.gov, DGIdb, ChEMBL, PubMed Literature, PubMed NP, ClinicalTrials.gov NP · Pipeline phase 7 · Updated 2026-07-05

Remission & chronicity

Spontaneous remission
Natural episodic pattern: untreated, median episode length 3-6 months; recurrence in 90% within 5 years; functional impairment between episodes in 30-60%; suicide risk 20-30x general population
Best-intervention remission
Lithium reduces recurrence risk by 30-40% and suicide risk by 60-70% (unique anti-suicidal effect not shared by other mood stabilisers); combination pharmacotherapy + structured psychotherapy (IPSRT, CBT): 60-70% euthymia maintenance at 2 years in first-episode BD
Drug-free remission
—
Relapse after remission
—
Chronicity
—
Gap size
Moderate — lithium underuse is a genuine gap; psychotherapy chronically under-integrated with pharmacotherapy
Primary barrier
practice/framing
Barrier detail: Lithium is the most evidence-based mood stabiliser with the only demonstrated anti-suicidal effect but is declining in use relative to newer anticonvulsants and antipsychotics partly due to monitoring requirements; many clinicians avoid lithium due to perceived complexity despite its superior evidence base

Last SoC change: 2023 — NICE/APA guidelines reaffirm lithium as first-line for BD prevention; 2019 cariprazine and lumateperone approved; atypical antipsychotics (quetiapine, olanzapine, aripiprazole) as mood stabilisers since 2000s; lithium first used 1949

Remission data layers: disease_db_100 · source-locked manual data · confidence: high

Related: Intervention atlas

Identifiers: MONDO: MONDO:0004985 · ORPHA: ORPHA:1713

Registry trials

  • NCT00451542 — Divalproex vs. Lamotrigine for Bipolar Disorder (TERMINATED)
  • NCT00667745 — Effectiveness of Lithium Plus Optimized Medication in Treating People With Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00332098 — Effectiveness of Family-Focused Treatment Plus Pharmacotherapy for Bipolar Disorder in Adolescents (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00204503 — Open-Label Depakote ER in Patients With Bipolar I or II Depression and Alcohol Abuse or Dependence (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT00240110 — Valproate Efficacy in Cocaine-Bipolar Comorbidity (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT00139074 — Seroquel in Acute Mania: Study to Investigate if Valproate Add-On Therapy is Superior to Quetiapine Monotherapy in Acutely Manic Patients (TERMINATED) PHASE4
  • NCT00203528 — Divalproex ER vs. Risperidone for Bipolar Disorder With Comorbid Substance Use Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01588457 — Sequential Multiple Assignment Treatment for Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Treatments for late-life bipolar disorder (The American journal of geriatric pharmacotherapy, 2006)
  • Clinical trial publication Fixed Dose Combination Tablets of Aripiprazole and Divalproex Sodium: a Pilot Pharmacokinetic Study in Human Volunteers (AAPS PharmSciTech, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Depression and Suicidality Outcomes in the Treatment of Early Age Mania Study (Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Risperidone and divalproex differentially engage the fronto-striato-temporal circuitry in pediatric mania: a pharmacological functional magnetic resonance imaging study (Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication A pilot pharmacotherapy trial for depressed youths at high genetic risk for bipolarity (Journal of child and adolescent psychopharmacology, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Open-label steady-state pharmacokinetic drug interaction study on co-administered quetiapine fumarate and divalproex sodium in patients with schizophrenia, schizoaffective disorder, or bipolar disorder (Human psychopharmacology, 2007)
  • Randomized controlled trial Double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of divalproex monotherapy in the treatment of symptomatic youth at high risk for developing bipolar disorder (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2007)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Randomised controlled trials of mood stabilisers for people with autism spectrum disorder: systematic review and meta-analysis (BJPsych open, 2022)
  • Cochrane review [Anticonvulsants in the treatment of alcoholism] (Fortschritte der Neurologie-Psychiatrie, 2009)
  • Systematic review Levetiracetam for bipolar disorder: Consideration from a systematic review (Bipolar disorders, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Investigating the efficacy of levetiracetam as an adjunct to olanzapine for acute manic episodes of bipolar disorder: A double-blind placebo-controlled randomized clinical trial (Journal of affective disorders, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Levetiracetam as an Adjunctive Treatment for Mania: A Double-Blind, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Trial (Neuropsychobiology, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Levetiracetam adjunct to quetiapine for the acute manic phase of bipolar disorder: a randomized, double-blind and placebo-controlled clinical trial of efficacy, safety and tolerability (International clinical psychopharmacology, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Levetiracetam in the management of bipolar depression: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2011)
  • Clinical trial publication Preliminary observations on the effectiveness of levetiracetam in the open adjunctive treatment of refractory bipolar disorder (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2005)

8 trials 13 Cochrane 3 Meta-analysis 20 Trial pubs 7 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT00035802 — A Study to Evaluate the Safety and Effectiveness of Topiramate Compared to Placebo in the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar I Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00035230 — A Study on the Safety and Efficacy of Topiramate in the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar I Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00037674 — A Study on the Safety and Efficacy of Topiramate in the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar I Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00572117 — Adjunctive Topiramate for Treatment of Alcohol Dependence in Patients With Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00240721 — A Study of the Effectiveness and Safety of Topiramate in the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01000402 — Juvenile Bipolar Disorder Outpatient Program (UNKNOWN) PHASE4
  • NCT00986336 — A Pharmacokinetic Study of Risperidone and Topiramate Administered Alone and in Combination in Patients With Bipolar Disorder or Schizoaffective Disorders (COMPLETED) PHASE1
  • NCT07213466 — Individualized Pharmacological Approach to Obesity in Patients With Bipolar Disorder (RECRUITING) PHASE4

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Therapeutic Overlap Between Bipolar Disorder and Migraine: A Systematic Review of Pharmacological Trials (Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland), 2026)
  • Cochrane review Randomised controlled trials of mood stabilisers for people with autism spectrum disorder: systematic review and meta-analysis (BJPsych open, 2022)
  • Cochrane review Pharmacological Treatment for Comorbid Bipolar Disorder and Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder in Adults (Journal of psychiatric practice, 2020)
  • Cochrane review Influence of the use of atypical antipsychotics in metabolic syndrome (Revista espanola de sanidad penitenciaria, 2020)
  • Cochrane review Lithium for acute mania (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2019)
  • Meta-analysis Systematic Review and Network Meta-Analysis: Efficacy and Safety of Antipsychotics vs Antiepileptics or Lithium for Acute Mania in Children and Adolescents (Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis [The use of anticonvulsants and the levels of folate, vitamin B12 and homocysteine] (Tijdschrift voor psychiatrie, 2018)
  • Meta-analysis Pharmacologic and behavioral interventions to improve cardiovascular risk factors in adults with serious mental illness: a systematic review and meta-analysis (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2014)
  • Systematic review What not to use in bipolar disorders: A systematic review of non-recommended treatments in clinical practice guidelines (Journal of affective disorders, 2022)
  • Systematic review Adverse outcomes during pregnancy and major congenital malformations in infants of patients with bipolar and schizoaffective disorders treated with antiepileptic drugs: A systematic review (Psychiatria polska, 2019)
  • Clinical trial publication A Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Pilot Study of Topiramate in Manic Adolescents Treated with Olanzapine (Journal of child and adolescent psychopharmacology, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication A randomized, placebo-controlled proof-of-concept trial of adjunctive topiramate for alcohol use disorders in bipolar disorder (The American journal on addictions, 2016)

  • NCT00240110 — Valproate Efficacy in Cocaine-Bipolar Comorbidity (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT00139074 — Seroquel in Acute Mania: Study to Investigate if Valproate Add-On Therapy is Superior to Quetiapine Monotherapy in Acutely Manic Patients (TERMINATED) PHASE4
  • NCT00203528 — Divalproex ER vs. Risperidone for Bipolar Disorder With Comorbid Substance Use Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00477373 — Gulf Evaluation of VAlproate (Depakine Chrono) in maNia Study (COMPLETED) PHASE4

Published literature

  • Systematic review Pharmacotherapies for co-occurring substance use and bipolar disorders: A systematic review (Bipolar disorders, 2019)
  • Systematic review Risks of neurobehavioral teratogenicity associated with prenatal exposure to valproate monotherapy: a systematic review with regulatory repercussions (CNS spectrums, 2014)
  • Clinical trial publication [Open randomized comparative twelve-week study of lithium and valproate in manic episode] (Zhurnal nevrologii i psikhiatrii imeni S.S. Korsakova, 2009)
  • Clinical trial publication Valproate, bipolar disorder and polycystic ovarian syndrome (Bipolar disorders, 2003)

CT.gov search · Cochrane · PubMed

 DGIdb ChEMBL Open Targets
VALPROIC ACID Repurposing Small molecule Approved Strong 95 Varies — check openprescribing.net (UK) or costplusdrugs.com / GoodRx (US)
8 trials 11 Cochrane 3 Meta-analysis 38 Trial pubs 19 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT00177567 — Treatment of Geriatric Bipolar Mood Disorders: A Pilot Study (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00451542 — Divalproex vs. Lamotrigine for Bipolar Disorder (TERMINATED)
  • NCT00667745 — Effectiveness of Lithium Plus Optimized Medication in Treating People With Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00332098 — Effectiveness of Family-Focused Treatment Plus Pharmacotherapy for Bipolar Disorder in Adolescents (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00204503 — Open-Label Depakote ER in Patients With Bipolar I or II Depression and Alcohol Abuse or Dependence (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT00562861 — Treatment for Bipolar Depression: Acute & Prophylactic Efficacy With Citalopram (COMPLETED) PHASE2, PHASE3
  • NCT01064297 — Lamotrigine Pregnancy Registry (LAM05) (COMPLETED)
  • NCT00240110 — Valproate Efficacy in Cocaine-Bipolar Comorbidity (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Therapeutic Overlap Between Bipolar Disorder and Migraine: A Systematic Review of Pharmacological Trials (Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland), 2026)
  • Cochrane review Mechanisms of weight gain associated with valproic acid: a scoping review (European archives of psychiatry and clinical neuroscience, 2026)
  • Cochrane review [Lamotrigine in bipolar disorder: preventing depression to treat the disease] (Rivista di psichiatria, 2021)
  • Cochrane review A Review of the Pharmacological and Clinical Profile of Newer Atypical Antipsychotics as Treatments for Bipolar Disorder: Considerations for Use in Older Patients (Drugs & aging, 2018)
  • Cochrane review [The effects of pharmacological and psychotherapeutic treatment of comorbid anxiety disorders in patients with bipolar disorder] (Tijdschrift voor psychiatrie, 2018)
  • Meta-analysis [The use of anticonvulsants and the levels of folate, vitamin B12 and homocysteine] (Tijdschrift voor psychiatrie, 2018)
  • Meta-analysis Maintenance therapy with second generation antipsychotics for bipolar disorder - A systematic review and meta-analysis (Journal of affective disorders, 2017)
  • Meta-analysis Valproic acid, valproate and divalproex in the maintenance treatment of bipolar disorder (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2013)
  • Systematic review Lamotrigine-induced Stevens-Johnson syndrome: a systematic review of case reports and case series (Clinical toxicology (Philadelphia, Pa.), 2026)
  • Systematic review Clozapine-induced cardiac toxicity and successful rechallenge in children and adolescents: a systematic review and new case report (Psychiatry research, 2025)
  • Systematic review Evaluating the efficacy of valproic acid in alcohol use disorder: a systematic analysis of clinical trials from ClinicalTrials.gov (Frontiers in pharmacology, 2025)
  • Systematic review Exploring accelerated aging as a target of bipolar disorder treatment: A systematic review (Journal of psychiatric research, 2024)

8 trials 1 Cochrane 36 Trial pubs 18 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT00451542 — Divalproex vs. Lamotrigine for Bipolar Disorder (TERMINATED)
  • NCT00667745 — Effectiveness of Lithium Plus Optimized Medication in Treating People With Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00332098 — Effectiveness of Family-Focused Treatment Plus Pharmacotherapy for Bipolar Disorder in Adolescents (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00204503 — Open-Label Depakote ER in Patients With Bipolar I or II Depression and Alcohol Abuse or Dependence (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT00240110 — Valproate Efficacy in Cocaine-Bipolar Comorbidity (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT00139074 — Seroquel in Acute Mania: Study to Investigate if Valproate Add-On Therapy is Superior to Quetiapine Monotherapy in Acutely Manic Patients (TERMINATED) PHASE4
  • NCT00203528 — Divalproex ER vs. Risperidone for Bipolar Disorder With Comorbid Substance Use Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01588457 — Sequential Multiple Assignment Treatment for Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Treatments for late-life bipolar disorder (The American journal of geriatric pharmacotherapy, 2006)
  • Clinical trial publication Fixed Dose Combination Tablets of Aripiprazole and Divalproex Sodium: a Pilot Pharmacokinetic Study in Human Volunteers (AAPS PharmSciTech, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Depression and Suicidality Outcomes in the Treatment of Early Age Mania Study (Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Risperidone and divalproex differentially engage the fronto-striato-temporal circuitry in pediatric mania: a pharmacological functional magnetic resonance imaging study (Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry, 2012)
  • Clinical trial publication A pilot pharmacotherapy trial for depressed youths at high genetic risk for bipolarity (Journal of child and adolescent psychopharmacology, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Open-label steady-state pharmacokinetic drug interaction study on co-administered quetiapine fumarate and divalproex sodium in patients with schizophrenia, schizoaffective disorder, or bipolar disorder (Human psychopharmacology, 2007)
  • Randomized controlled trial Double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of divalproex monotherapy in the treatment of symptomatic youth at high risk for developing bipolar disorder (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2007)

Registry trials

  • NCT00015769 — Pilot Study of Levetiracetam (Keppra® (Registered Trademark)) for Bipolar Illness (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT00566150 — Levetiracetam in the Management of Bipolar Depression (COMPLETED) NA

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Randomised controlled trials of mood stabilisers for people with autism spectrum disorder: systematic review and meta-analysis (BJPsych open, 2022)
  • Cochrane review [Anticonvulsants in the treatment of alcoholism] (Fortschritte der Neurologie-Psychiatrie, 2009)
  • Systematic review Levetiracetam for bipolar disorder: Consideration from a systematic review (Bipolar disorders, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Investigating the efficacy of levetiracetam as an adjunct to olanzapine for acute manic episodes of bipolar disorder: A double-blind placebo-controlled randomized clinical trial (Journal of affective disorders, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Levetiracetam as an Adjunctive Treatment for Mania: A Double-Blind, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Trial (Neuropsychobiology, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Levetiracetam adjunct to quetiapine for the acute manic phase of bipolar disorder: a randomized, double-blind and placebo-controlled clinical trial of efficacy, safety and tolerability (International clinical psychopharmacology, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Levetiracetam in the management of bipolar depression: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2011)
  • Clinical trial publication Preliminary observations on the effectiveness of levetiracetam in the open adjunctive treatment of refractory bipolar disorder (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2005)

View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT00035802 — A Study to Evaluate the Safety and Effectiveness of Topiramate Compared to Placebo in the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar I Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00035230 — A Study on the Safety and Efficacy of Topiramate in the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar I Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00037674 — A Study on the Safety and Efficacy of Topiramate in the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar I Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00572117 — Adjunctive Topiramate for Treatment of Alcohol Dependence in Patients With Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00240721 — A Study of the Effectiveness and Safety of Topiramate in the Treatment of Patients With Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01000402 — Juvenile Bipolar Disorder Outpatient Program (UNKNOWN) PHASE4
  • NCT00986336 — A Pharmacokinetic Study of Risperidone and Topiramate Administered Alone and in Combination in Patients With Bipolar Disorder or Schizoaffective Disorders (COMPLETED) PHASE1
  • NCT07213466 — Individualized Pharmacological Approach to Obesity in Patients With Bipolar Disorder (RECRUITING) PHASE4

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Therapeutic Overlap Between Bipolar Disorder and Migraine: A Systematic Review of Pharmacological Trials (Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland), 2026)
  • Cochrane review Randomised controlled trials of mood stabilisers for people with autism spectrum disorder: systematic review and meta-analysis (BJPsych open, 2022)
  • Cochrane review Pharmacological Treatment for Comorbid Bipolar Disorder and Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder in Adults (Journal of psychiatric practice, 2020)
  • Cochrane review Influence of the use of atypical antipsychotics in metabolic syndrome (Revista espanola de sanidad penitenciaria, 2020)
  • Cochrane review Lithium for acute mania (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2019)
  • Meta-analysis Systematic Review and Network Meta-Analysis: Efficacy and Safety of Antipsychotics vs Antiepileptics or Lithium for Acute Mania in Children and Adolescents (Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis [The use of anticonvulsants and the levels of folate, vitamin B12 and homocysteine] (Tijdschrift voor psychiatrie, 2018)
  • Meta-analysis Pharmacologic and behavioral interventions to improve cardiovascular risk factors in adults with serious mental illness: a systematic review and meta-analysis (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2014)
  • Systematic review What not to use in bipolar disorders: A systematic review of non-recommended treatments in clinical practice guidelines (Journal of affective disorders, 2022)
  • Systematic review Adverse outcomes during pregnancy and major congenital malformations in infants of patients with bipolar and schizoaffective disorders treated with antiepileptic drugs: A systematic review (Psychiatria polska, 2019)
  • Clinical trial publication A Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Pilot Study of Topiramate in Manic Adolescents Treated with Olanzapine (Journal of child and adolescent psychopharmacology, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication A randomized, placebo-controlled proof-of-concept trial of adjunctive topiramate for alcohol use disorders in bipolar disorder (The American journal on addictions, 2016)

Published literature

  • Systematic review Pharmacotherapies for co-occurring substance use and bipolar disorders: A systematic review (Bipolar disorders, 2019)
  • Systematic review Risks of neurobehavioral teratogenicity associated with prenatal exposure to valproate monotherapy: a systematic review with regulatory repercussions (CNS spectrums, 2014)
  • Clinical trial publication [Open randomized comparative twelve-week study of lithium and valproate in manic episode] (Zhurnal nevrologii i psikhiatrii imeni S.S. Korsakova, 2009)
  • Clinical trial publication Valproate, bipolar disorder and polycystic ovarian syndrome (Bipolar disorders, 2003)

VALPROIC ACID Repurposing via ALDH5A1 Small molecule Approved Strong 95 Varies — check openprescribing.net (UK) or costplusdrugs.com / GoodRx (US)
8 trials 11 Cochrane 3 Meta-analysis 38 Trial pubs 19 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT00177567 — Treatment of Geriatric Bipolar Mood Disorders: A Pilot Study (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00451542 — Divalproex vs. Lamotrigine for Bipolar Disorder (TERMINATED)
  • NCT00667745 — Effectiveness of Lithium Plus Optimized Medication in Treating People With Bipolar Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00332098 — Effectiveness of Family-Focused Treatment Plus Pharmacotherapy for Bipolar Disorder in Adolescents (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00204503 — Open-Label Depakote ER in Patients With Bipolar I or II Depression and Alcohol Abuse or Dependence (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT00562861 — Treatment for Bipolar Depression: Acute & Prophylactic Efficacy With Citalopram (COMPLETED) PHASE2, PHASE3
  • NCT01064297 — Lamotrigine Pregnancy Registry (LAM05) (COMPLETED)
  • NCT00240110 — Valproate Efficacy in Cocaine-Bipolar Comorbidity (COMPLETED) PHASE1, PHASE2

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Therapeutic Overlap Between Bipolar Disorder and Migraine: A Systematic Review of Pharmacological Trials (Pharmaceuticals (Basel, Switzerland), 2026)
  • Cochrane review Mechanisms of weight gain associated with valproic acid: a scoping review (European archives of psychiatry and clinical neuroscience, 2026)
  • Cochrane review [Lamotrigine in bipolar disorder: preventing depression to treat the disease] (Rivista di psichiatria, 2021)
  • Cochrane review A Review of the Pharmacological and Clinical Profile of Newer Atypical Antipsychotics as Treatments for Bipolar Disorder: Considerations for Use in Older Patients (Drugs & aging, 2018)
  • Cochrane review [The effects of pharmacological and psychotherapeutic treatment of comorbid anxiety disorders in patients with bipolar disorder] (Tijdschrift voor psychiatrie, 2018)
  • Meta-analysis [The use of anticonvulsants and the levels of folate, vitamin B12 and homocysteine] (Tijdschrift voor psychiatrie, 2018)
  • Meta-analysis Maintenance therapy with second generation antipsychotics for bipolar disorder - A systematic review and meta-analysis (Journal of affective disorders, 2017)
  • Meta-analysis Valproic acid, valproate and divalproex in the maintenance treatment of bipolar disorder (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2013)
  • Systematic review Lamotrigine-induced Stevens-Johnson syndrome: a systematic review of case reports and case series (Clinical toxicology (Philadelphia, Pa.), 2026)
  • Systematic review Clozapine-induced cardiac toxicity and successful rechallenge in children and adolescents: a systematic review and new case report (Psychiatry research, 2025)
  • Systematic review Evaluating the efficacy of valproic acid in alcohol use disorder: a systematic analysis of clinical trials from ClinicalTrials.gov (Frontiers in pharmacology, 2025)
  • Systematic review Exploring accelerated aging as a target of bipolar disorder treatment: A systematic review (Journal of psychiatric research, 2024)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Omega-3 Fatty Acids for the Treatment of Bipolar Disorder Symptoms: A Narrative Review of the Current Clinical Evidence (Marine drugs, 2025)
  • Cochrane review A Systematic Review of Nutraceuticals for the Treatment of Bipolar Disorder (Canadian journal of psychiatry. Revue canadienne de psychiatrie, 2021)
  • Cochrane review Role of omega-3 fatty acids in the treatment of depressive disorders: a comprehensive meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials (PloS one, 2014)
  • Cochrane review Dietary supplements for preventing postnatal depression (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2013)
  • Cochrane review Omega-3 fatty acids for bipolar disorder (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2008)
  • Meta-analysis Clinician guidelines for the treatment of psychiatric disorders with nutraceuticals and phytoceuticals: The World Federation of Societies of Biological Psychiatry (WFSBP) and Canadian Network for Mood and Anxiety Treatments (CANMAT) Taskforce (The world journal of biological psychiatry : the official journal of the World Federation of Societies of Biological Psychiatry, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Omega-3 fatty acids for treating residual depressive symptoms in adult patients with bipolar disorder: A systematic review and meta-analysis of double-blind randomized, placebo-controlled trials (Bipolar disorders, 2021)
  • Meta-analysis The Psychoneuroimmunological Role of Omega-3 Polyunsaturated Fatty Acids in Major Depressive Disorder and Bipolar Disorder (Advances in mind-body medicine, 2017)
  • Meta-analysis Meta-analysis of erythrocyte polyunsaturated fatty acid biostatus in bipolar disorder (Bipolar disorders, 2016)
  • Clinical trial publication Supplementation of Omega-3 Increases Serum Levels of Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor and Decreases Depression Status in Patients With Bipolar Disorder: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial (Journal of human nutrition and dietetics : the official journal of the British Dietetic Association, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication A 52-week prophylactic randomised control trial of omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids in bipolar disorder (Bipolar disorders, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Moody kids years later: Long-term outcomes of youth from the Omega-3 and therapy (OATS) studies (Journal of affective disorders, 2021)

Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
